title: question (one JSON object per file)
description: One question per .json file under <domain>/questions/ — the structured source of truth read by src/engine/store.ts (parseQuestionJson). Human/Obsidian-facing markdown is produced on demand by the exporter, not stored here. `templates/question.json` is the canonical example.

source.description: primary-source URLs (required by policy; questions are generated from sources, not copied)

q.description: the question prompt (Japanese for AWS; '日本語の指示 + 対象英文' for English)

choices.description: present for single_choice / multi only; each item starts with its letter, e.g. 'A. …'. null/absent otherwise.

answer.description: single_choice: the correct letter ('B'); multi: comma-joined letters ('A,C'); cloze/translation/free: the model answer text.

explanation.description: key points in your own words (no source copy-paste); for choices, also why the others are wrong.

hint.description: optional hint shown on demand (never the answer). null/absent if none — the app can ask Claude for one at study time.

speak.description: optional text the app reads aloud with `say` (target-language sentence). null/absent for non-language questions.

answer_ruby.description: optional per-word katakana ruby for an English answer: array of [word, katakana] pairs, e.g. [["Mai","マイ"],["drinks","ドリンクス"]]. Rendered as furigana over the answer. null/absent for non-English answers.

answer_full.description: cloze only: the complete model sentence with each blank's fill wrapped in [[...]], e.g. 'Our meeting starts [[at]] nine o'clock [[on]] Monday morning.'. The app shows this (with the [[...]] parts highlighted) as the model answer for cloze. null/absent for non-cloze.
